pattern: Counting queen placements is depth-first backtracking row by row, where three boolean sets of occupied columns and diagonals make each conflict check constant time.
hints: Place exactly one queen per row, so the search only ever chooses a column for the current row.
Two queens attack along a column, a down-right diagonal (row minus col constant) or a down-left diagonal (row plus col constant).
Recurse row by row trying each free column, marking the column and both diagonals as used; count one whenever you place past the last row.

edge_inputs.description: single cell has one trivial solution
three by three also has no solution

stress.description: deterministically sweep every legal board size
stress.note: n is tiny by constraint so the full backtracking search is cheap
